title: sphalmium racemosum
intro: Sphalmium racemosum, also known as the cluster spleenwort, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. It is native to North America and is commonly found in woodland areas. Sphalmium racemosum has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is often used as a treatment for fever, sore throats, and coughs, as well as to promote digestion and reduce stress.
